Specification: Can UGA provide useful cross-modality evidence even when SBS, DBS, and indel exposures are solved separately?
520 tumors with sparse mixtures of 12 latent mutational processes.
SBS96, DBS78, and ID83 standard channel universes plus event-level 48D UGA clean-context vectors.
Atlas model compact_event_legacy_d10; layout [L context, REF payload, R context, ALT payload].
NNLS is fit independently for each modality using either categorical signatures or UGA signature centroids.
All SBS/DBS/ID events are collapsed into a single burden-weighted 48D mean and fit against all modality-specific UGA centroids at once.
UGA centroids are used for cross-modality nearest-neighbor retrieval and SBS-to-DBS/ID held-out exposure imputation.
SBS, DBS, and ID channels are unequal axes, so cross-catalog distance is undefined without external labels; chance/tie expectation is reported.

Label: Legacy zero padding
Notes: Current 2-bit payload with absent slots set to (0,0).

Label: Fractional pad sentinel
Notes: Uses (0.5,0.5) for every absent slot; injective but no longer binary bit identity.

Label: Out-of-range pad sentinel
Notes: Uses (-1,-1) for absent slots; injective but leaves the bounded UGA cube.

Label: Masked payload
Notes: Adds one presence bit per slot: [X,Y,M].

Label: Length-coded payload
Notes: Adds a block-level payload length code: [X,Y,L], with one overflow state.
